session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8544 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback data and then classifying it according to the specification.

tool_use.param: Get file restructuring context for current branch

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I have all the information needed to classify the PR feedback. Let me analyze the data according to the Comment Classification Model:
**Analysis:**
1. **PR Info:** - PR Number: 8544 - PR Title: "[erk-plan] Plan: Document mid-rebase behavior for `erk pr rebase`" - PR URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8544
2. **Review Threads Analysis:** - 1 review thread from human author (schrockn) - Path: `.erk/impl-context/plan.md` (line 22) - Content: "also make sure that the help string describes how this is an intelligent rebase with automatic conflict resolution" - Author is human (not a bot) - Not outdated
3. **File Restructuring Context:** - Only 2 files changed: `.erk/impl-context/plan.md` and `.erk/impl-context/ref.json` - These are additions, not restructuring (renames/moves) - No file-level restructuring detected
4. **Classification Decision:** - **Classification**: `actionable` - This is a code review comment requesting changes/clarifications to the help string - **Pre-existing**: `false` - Author is human, not a bot, so it doesn't qualify as pre-existing - **Complexity**: `local` - Single line change/clarification at a specific location (line 22 in one file)
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 8544, "pr_title": "[erk-plan] Plan: Document mid-rebase behavior for `erk pr rebase`", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8544", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xa_hs", "type": "review", "path": ".erk/impl-context/plan.md", "line": 22, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Update help string to describe intelligent rebase with automatic conflict resolution", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "also make sure that the help string describes how this is an intelligent rebase with automatic conflict resolution" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 0, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
